summary: Matched 11 previously stubbed item functions across Lugia, Oldkuri, Whitebea, Nessbat, Ness PK Thunder Ball, Matadogas, Hitodeman, Great Fox Laser, and Kirby_2F23 item code. The PR also refined item attribute and item-var layouts in itCommonItems.h and tightened two item header prototypes. decomp.dev reported 11 new 100% matches, +1336 matched bytes, and matched code rising to 56.03% (+0.03%).

key_files.role: Expanded Lugia, Hitodeman, and Whitebea attribute/item-var layouts so newly matched functions could address real fields instead of padding.
Matched it_802E4558 and itWhitebea_UnkMotion7_Anim; added local grIceMt_801FA6D8 prototype.
Matched it_802D4494 and expanded local attributes to expose x14/x18.
Matched it_802EB1EC and added it_2725.h include.
Matched it_802F2810 and added item subsystem includes.
Changed it_802D4494 prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void(Item_GObj*).
Changed it_802D1830 prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void(Item_GObj*).

what_changed: Replaced 11 placeholder comments with matched C definitions: itLugia_UnkMotion3_Phys, it_802D23F4, itOldkuri_UnkMotion1_Phys, itWhitebea_UnkMotion7_Anim, it_802E4558, it_2725_Logic58_PickedUp, it_802AB3F0, it_802CB798, it_802D4494, it_802EB1EC, and it_802F2810.
Expanded itLugiaAttributes from two f32 fields to five f32 fields through x10, and exposed itLugia_ItemVars.x60 before the existing Vec3 xE50 field.
Exposed itHitodeman_ItemVars.x84 and local Hitodeman attribute fields x14/x18, enabling it_802D4494 to set a randomized item-var value using HSD_Randi.
Expanded itWhiteBeaAttributes with pad_x8 and s16 x14, used by itWhitebea_UnkMotion7_Anim to initialize whitebea item vars when animation state ends.

Implemented two similar item init/setup patterns in Lugia and Matadogas: set xD44_lifeTimer from attributes, call it_80274740, start animation with Item_80268E5C, assign efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll hitlag callbacks, then call it_8026B3A8.
Implemented Great Fox Laser reset/transition logic: calls it_8027B730 and it_8027C56C, clears item flags/fields, calls it_8027542C, marks greatfoxlaser.x36, then transitions via it_802EB268.
Implemented Nessbat pickup behavior by OR-ing the four itcmd variables and selecting Item_80268E5C animation index 1 or 0 before Item_802694CC.

Implemented Ness PK Thunder Ball out-parameter helper it_802AB3F0 with null checks for gobj, user_data, and out; copies a Vec3 from pkthunder.xDEC using idx * 0x18 pointer stepping or zeros the output Vec3.
Implemented Kirby_2F23 item setup it_802F2810, including Item_80268E5C, setting ip->on_accessory = it_802F289C, and calling item helper routines through explicit Item_GObj casts.
smart_moves: The PR did not just write function bodies; it first corrected shared item struct layouts so field accesses landed at documented offsets, which is often the difference between plausible C and a real match.
Reusable lifecycle setup sequences were recognized across item files, especially the Lugia/Matadogas pattern of lifeTimer setup, animation update, hitlag callback assignment, and it_8026B3A8.
Several functions preserve exact stack behavior with PAD_STACK(8) or PAD_STACK(16), indicating attention to non-semantic stack layout requirements for matching.
The Ness PK Thunder Ball helper keeps defensive null behavior and explicit zeroing of x/y/z fallback output instead of simplifying away branch structure that likely matters for codegen.
Header prototypes were narrowed only where the PR had concrete matched evidence, replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for it_802D4494 and it_802D1830 with typed Item_GObj signatures.
The Whitebea match used a small local stage-function prototype, grIceMt_801FA6D8, avoiding a larger header refactor not evidenced by this PR.
decomp_lessons: When item functions reference xDD4_itemVar or article special-attribute offsets, update the relevant struct padding and offset comments before forcing casts or magic pointer arithmetic.

Look for repeated item init idioms across item modules: xD44_lifeTimer assignment, it_80274740, Item_80268E5C with ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE, efLib hitlag hooks, and it_8026B3A8 can form a recognizable matching template.
Do not prematurely semantic-name unknown fields; this PR kept x0/x4/x10/x14/x18/x60/x84-style names while still exposing the exact offsets needed for matched code.
Use GET_ITEM versus gobj->user_data according to the codegen pattern in the target function; this PR uses both forms in different matched functions.
Out-parameter helpers may require branch-preserving null handling and field assignment order to match, even when the logic could be simplified in normal C.
Small PAD_STACK calls and explicit casts between HSD_GObj* and Item_GObj* remain valid matching tools in item code.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions keep address-derived names such as it_802D23F4, it_802AB3F0, it_802CB798, it_802D4494, it_802EB1EC, and it_802F2810.
Motion callbacks follow item-name plus UnkMotion number plus phase, for example itLugia_UnkMotion3_Phys, itOldkuri_UnkMotion1_Phys, and itWhitebea_UnkMotion7_Anim.
Logic callback naming can include the shared item subsystem prefix and event, as in it_2725_Logic58_PickedUp.
Struct fields retain offset-style placeholder names and offset comments, such as itLugiaAttributes.x10, itLugia_ItemVars.x60, itHitodeman_ItemVars.x84, and itWhiteBeaAttributes.x14.
Headers preserve address comments beside prototypes, e.g. /* 2D4494 */ void it_802D4494(Item_GObj* gobj).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK(16) in it_802EB1EC and itWhitebea_UnkMotion7_Anim, and PAD_STACK(8) in it_802D4494, it_802CB798, and itOldkuri_UnkMotion1_Phys.
Preserved post-decrement control flow in itLugia_UnkMotion3_Phys: if (ip->xDD4_itemVar.lugia.x60-- != 0) set z velocity from attrs->x10, else zero velocity and call it_802D1830.
Used exact helper-call ordering for item setup matches, especially it_80274740 before Item_80268E5C and hitlag callback assignment before it_8026B3A8.
Used explicit bitfield and flag assignments in it_802EB1EC: xDCC_flag.b3 = 0, xDC8_word.flags.x15 = 0, and greatfoxlaser.x36 = 1.
Matched it_802AB3F0 with pointer arithmetic through (u8*) and idx * 0x18 before reading pkthunder.xDEC as a Vec3.
Kept explicit Item_GObj casts in it_802F2810 when calling item helpers from an HSD_GObj* parameter.
Hitodeman randomization uses HSD_Randi((s32)(attrs->x14 - attrs->x18)) and then adds attrs->x18 as f32 to store in hitodeman.x84.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported 11 new 100% matches and +1336 matched bytes for GALE01.
The bot also reported one unmatched-item improvement: ithitodeman .sdata2 improved from 37.50% to 57.89% (+11 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Search remaining item stubs for the lifeTimer + it_80274740 + Item_80268E5C + efLib hitlag + it_8026B3A8 init pattern.
Audit item headers for functions still declared UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS where matched neighboring code proves Item_GObj* signatures.
Search xDD4_itemVar padding in itCommonItems.h for fields that can be exposed by upcoming item matches.
Compare other Vec3 out-parameter helpers against it_802AB3F0 for null-check and zero-output branch structure.
Look for other Whitebea or stage-interaction functions that call grIceMt_801FA6D8 or similar local stage hooks.
